content: What did Daniel resolve in his heart not to do at Babylon?
explanation: Daniel 1:8 — Daniel kept his principles even in a foreign land. God blesses faithfulness.
options: Not learn the Babylonian language
Not defile himself with the king's food and wine
Not work
Not speak

content: What does Nebuchadnezzar's dream of the statue prophesy?
explanation: Daniel 2:44 — The stone cut without hands = God's kingdom that will crush all kingdoms and stand forever.
options: World empires and the eternal kingdom of God
About the near future
Only about Babylon
About war

content: Looking into the furnace, how many men did the king see instead of three?
explanation: Daniel 3:25 — The fourth person may be an angel or a manifestation of God. He is with His children in the fire.
options: 5 people
3 people
2 people
4 people, the fourth like a son of the gods

content: How was Nebuchadnezzar punished for his pride?
explanation: Daniel 4:33 — Seven periods living like a beast. When restored, the king praised God. A lesson in humility.
options: Lost his throne
Lived as a beast eating grass for seven periods until he acknowledged God's rule
Was exiled
Was killed

content: At King Belshazzar's feast, what appeared on the wall?
explanation: Daniel 5:5 — A hand writing: Mene, Mene, Tekel, Parsin. The kingdom has been weighed and found wanting.
options: A face
Fire
A hand writing on the wall
Light

content: When Daniel learned of the law forbidding prayer, what did he do?
explanation: Daniel 6:10 — Daniel did not change his prayer habit despite threats. Faith is not intimidated.
options: Prayed less
Prayed in secret
Continued to pray three times a day as before
Stopped praying

content: Daniel 7:13 sees 'one like a son of man' coming on the clouds of heaven. Who is this?
explanation: Jesus calls Himself the Son of Man (Matt 26:64) and quotes Daniel 7:13 about His return.
options: The angel Michael
Jesus Christ
Moses
Elijah

content: What event does Daniel's prophecy of seventy weeks (490 years) foretell?
explanation: Daniel 9:25-26 — 69 weeks (483 years) from the decree to rebuild until the Anointed One is cut off. Precisely fulfilled.
options: The restoration of the temple
The time of the Messiah's coming and being cut off
The end of the world
Pentecost

content: What does Daniel 12:2 prophesy?
explanation: Daniel 12:2 — One of the clearest prophecies of resurrection in the Old Testament.
options: There is no resurrection
Resurrection: many will rise — some to everlasting life, some to shame and everlasting contempt
Only spiritual resurrection
Only resurrection of the righteous

content: Why was the angel coming to Daniel delayed for 21 days?
explanation: Daniel 10:13 — There is a spiritual battle behind everything. Prayer affects the unseen world.
options: God assigned other work
He lost his way
Hindered by the prince of Persia (a spiritual power)
Unknown

content: What was the LORD's final word to Daniel?
explanation: Daniel 12:13 — A promise of resurrection and reward for the faithful. Hope for every believer.
options: Tell others
Go your way until the end. You shall rest, and shall stand in your allotted place
Wait
Write more

content: The three Hebrew young men refused to worship the king's golden image. What did they say when threatened with the fiery furnace?
explanation: Daniel 3:17-18 — Unconditional faith: trusting God can deliver, but remaining faithful no matter the outcome.
options: Spare us
Our God is able to deliver us, but if not, we will not worship the image
We will pray
God will deliver us

content: Into what was Daniel cast for refusing to stop praying?
explanation: Daniel 6:16 — Daniel was cast into the lions' den, but God sent His angel to shut the lions' mouths. Faithful to God whatever the cost.
options: A dungeon
A furnace
A pit
The lions' den

content: What did Nebuchadnezzar require of the wise men regarding his dream?
explanation: Daniel 2:5-6 — 'The king said, If you do not make known to me the dream and its interpretation, you shall be torn limb from limb.' He demanded both the dream itself and the interpretation.
options: Only interpret the dream
Guess the dream
Pray to the gods
Both tell the dream and interpret it

content: In the dream of the great statue, what was the head made of?
explanation: Daniel 2:32 — 'The head of this image was of fine gold, its chest and arms of silver, its middle and thighs of bronze.'
options: Bronze
Silver
Fine gold
Iron

content: How tall was the gold image King Nebuchadnezzar set up?
explanation: Daniel 3:1 — 'King Nebuchadnezzar made an image of gold, whose height was sixty cubits and its breadth six cubits.'
options: Ten cubits
Twenty cubits
Thirty cubits
Sixty cubits

content: What were the four words written on the wall at Belshazzar's feast?
explanation: Daniel 5:25 — 'And this is the writing that was inscribed: MENE, MENE, TEKEL, and PARSIN.'
options: Glory, glory, glory
Life, life, life, eternal
Mene, Mene, Tekel, Parsin
Wisdom, righteousness, holiness

content: How many satraps and how many high officials did King Darius set over the kingdom?
explanation: Daniel 6:1-2 — 'It pleased Darius to set over the kingdom 120 satraps... over them three high officials, of whom Daniel was one.'
options: 120 satraps and 3 high officials (Daniel was one)
70 satraps and 7 officials
12 satraps and 12 officials
100 satraps and 10 officials

content: In Daniel 8's vision, which two kingdoms do the ram and the goat represent?
explanation: Daniel 8:20-21 — 'As for the ram... it is the kings of Media and Persia. And the goat is the king of Greece.'
options: Egypt and Assyria
Babylon and Persia
Rome and Persia
Medo-Persia and Greece

content: Daniel 11:31 prophesies what will be set up in the holy place?
explanation: Daniel 11:31 — 'They shall... take away the regular burnt offering. And they shall set up the abomination that makes desolate.'
options: A new gold altar
The Ark of the Covenant
The abomination that makes desolate
A new seven-branched lampstand

content: According to Daniel 1:1, which Babylonian king besieged Jerusalem in the third year of Jehoiakim king of Judah?
explanation: Daniel 1:1 — 'In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah, Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon came to Jerusalem and besieged it.' First exile (~605 BC) — Daniel + 3 friends (Hananiah, Mishael, Azariah) were taken to Babylon with this group.
options: Belshazzar
Darius
NEBUCHADNEZZAR
Cyrus

content: According to Daniel 1:7, the chief gave Babylonian names to Daniel and 3 friends — what was Daniel's?
explanation: Daniel 1:7 — 'And the chief of the eunuchs gave them names: Daniel he called Belteshazzar, Hananiah he called Shadrach, Mishael he called Meshach, and Azariah he called Abednego.' Hebrew names contained EL/IAH (God) — changed to Babylonian names with pagan deity names. Purpose: cultural assimilation.
options: Abednego
Meshach
Shadrach
BELTESHAZZAR

content: According to Daniel 2:19, after Daniel's prayer, the MYSTERY of the king's dream was revealed to him in what?
explanation: Daniel 2:19 — 'Then the mystery was revealed to Daniel in a vision of the night. Then Daniel blessed the God of heaven.' This is the start of chapter 2's narrative: Nebuchadnezzar demanded the wise men retell his dream + interpret it, under penalty of death. Only Daniel prayed and was shown by God.
options: A NIGHT VISION
A noon nap
While reading
Through meditation

content: According to Daniel 3:27, after the 3 men exited the fiery furnace, what did the officials see?
explanation: Daniel 3:27 — 'And the satraps... saw that the fire had not had any power over the bodies of those men. The hair of their heads was not singed, their cloaks were not harmed, and no smell of fire had come upon them.' A clear miracle: not just escaped death — NO trace. King Nebuchadnezzar acknowledges the true God (3:28-29).
options: Charred (per the textual narrative)
Fire had NO power on their bodies — HAIR not singed, CLOTHES unchanged, NO smell of fire
Mildly burned (as described in the passage)
Vaporized (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Daniel 4:4-5, Nebuchadnezzar was at his palace when what happened?
explanation: Daniel 4:4-5 — 'I, Nebuchadnezzar, was at ease in my house and prospering in my palace. I saw a dream that made me afraid. As I lay in bed the fancies and the visions of my head alarmed me.' Chapter 4 opening — the dream of a GREAT TREE cut down — foretelling Nebuchadnezzar will be reduced to a beast for 7 years.
options: A messenger came
Heart attack
Thunder (per the textual narrative)
He had a DREAM that FRIGHTENED him

content: According to Daniel 5:1, which king (last of Babylon) held a great feast for 1,000 lords?
explanation: Daniel 5:1 — 'King Belshazzar made a great feast for a thousand of his lords and drank wine in front of the thousand.' Belshazzar = co-regent with Nabonidus (his father) ~553-539 BC. The feast was on the night Babylon fell (5:30 — the king was slain, Darius the Mede took over). Lavish feast as Persian + Median armies besieged.
options: Nebuchadnezzar
Darius
BELSHAZZAR
Cyrus

content: According to Daniel 6:4-5, the officials sought GROUNDS to accuse Daniel — what did they find?
explanation: Daniel 6:4-5 — 'Then the high officials and the satraps sought to find a ground for complaint against Daniel with regard to the kingdom, but they could find no ground for complaint or any fault, because he was faithful, and no error or fault was found in him. Then these men said, "We shall not find any ground for complaint against this Daniel unless we find it in connection with the law of his God."' Daniel = a model of integrity.
options: Found NO fault — Daniel was faithful; had to find pretext about his GOD'S LAW
Found corruption (as described in the passage)
Found lies (per the textual narrative)
Found rebellion (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Daniel 9:3, how did Daniel seek God in prayer for his people?
explanation: Daniel 9:3 — 'Then I turned my face to the Lord God, seeking him by prayer and pleas for mercy with fasting and sackcloth and ashes.' After reading Jeremiah (70-year exile — Jer 25:11-12, 29:10), Daniel prayed. 'SACKCLOTH + ASHES' = signs of repentance. Israel's restoration was not automatic — it required earnest prayer.
options: Silent reasoning (as described in the passage)
With offerings (per the textual narrative)
With formality (according to the text's framing)
Sought God with PRAYER, PLEAS, FASTING, sackcloth, and ASHES

content: According to Daniel 10:5-6, after 3 weeks of fasting, Daniel saw a MAN clothed how? Face and eyes like what?
explanation: Daniel 10:5-6 — 'I lifted up my eyes and looked, and behold, a man clothed in linen, with a belt of fine gold from Uphaz around his waist. His body was like beryl, his face like the appearance of lightning, his eyes like flaming torches, his arms and legs like the gleam of burnished bronze, and the sound of his words like the sound of a multitude.' This figure is possibly the pre-incarnate Christ (theophany) or a high-ranking angel. Rev 1:13-15 uses the same imagery for the glorified Christ.
options: Red robe — face like silver (per the textual narrative)
Clothed in LINEN, waist belted with gold of UPHAZ — face like LIGHTNING, EYES like flaming torches
Plain white — face like moon (according to the text's framing)
Black robe — face like night (as described in the passage)

content: According to Daniel 1:12-15, what test did Daniel propose to keep kosher?
explanation: Daniel 1:12-15 — 'Test your servants for ten days; let us be given vegetables to eat and water to drink. Then let our appearance and the appearance of the youths who eat the king's food be observed by you, and deal with your servants according to what you see.' So he listened to them in this matter, and tested them for ten days. At the end of ten days it was seen that they were better in appearance and fatter in flesh than all the youths who ate the king's food.' Daniel refused defilement by king's food (likely dedicated to pagan gods).
options: 10 DAYS of only VEGETABLES + WATER — then compare with those eating king's delicacies
1 year of fasting then judge (according to the text's framing)
30 days abstaining then compete (as described in the passage)
Just drink water and wait (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Daniel 1:17, what 2 gifts did God give the 4 Hebrews? Especially Daniel?
explanation: Daniel 1:17 — 'As for these four youths, God gave them learning and skill in all literature and wisdom, and Daniel had understanding in all visions and dreams.' Daniel's distinction: dream interpretation (like Joseph). Used in chapter 2 (Nebuchadnezzar's dream) and chapter 4 (tree dream).
options: Strength + courage — Daniel mighty (according to the text's framing)
Rule + strategy — Daniel a general (as described in the passage)
LEARNING + SKILL in all LITERATURE + WISDOM; especially Daniel had UNDERSTANDING in all VISIONS and DREAMS
Health + longevity — Daniel old (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Daniel 2:21, what does God (whom Daniel blesses) do with TIMES and KINGS?
explanation: Daniel 2:21 — 'He changes times and seasons; he removes kings and sets up kings; he gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding.' Daniel's praise after receiving the vision. KEY verse of OT political theology: GOD rules KINGS — not the king's pagan gods. Repeated many times in the book.
options: Keeps them unchanged (according to the text's framing)
CHANGES times and seasons — REMOVES KINGS and SETS UP KINGS — gives wisdom to the wise
Lets humans rule (per the textual narrative)
Only controls angels (as described in the passage)

content: According to Daniel 2:38, what does Daniel tell Nebuchadnezzar he is in the statue?
explanation: Daniel 2:38 — 'You are the head of gold.' King Nebuchadnezzar = head of gold = Babylonian kingdom. Next: silver chest (Medo-Persia), bronze belly (Greece), iron legs (Rome) — the Stone (eternal kingdom = Messiah) crushes the statue. This vision summarizes history.
options: The arms of silver
Belly + thighs of bronze
Legs of iron
The HEAD of GOLD

content: According to Daniel 3:4-6, what does Nebuchadnezzar command at the music? Punishment for refusal?
explanation: Daniel 3:4-6 — 'Then the herald proclaimed aloud, "You are commanded, O peoples, nations, and languages, that when you hear the sound of the horn, pipe... you are to fall down and worship the golden image that King Nebuchadnezzar has set up. And whoever does not fall down and worship shall immediately be cast into a burning fiery furnace."' Loyalty test: Daniel's 3 friends (Shadrach, Meshach, Abednego) refused.
options: FALL DOWN and WORSHIP the gold image — refusal = thrown into FIERY FURNACE
Stand still — or jail (according to the text's framing)
Sing along — or lose post (as described in the passage)
Pay tax — or fine (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Daniel 3:24-25, why was Nebuchadnezzar astonished looking into the furnace?
explanation: Daniel 3:24-25 — 'Then King Nebuchadnezzar was astonished and rose up in haste. He declared to his counselors, "Did we not cast three men bound into the fire?" They answered and said to the king, "True, O king." He answered and said, "But I see four men unbound, walking in the midst of the fire, and they are not hurt; and the appearance of the fourth is like a son of the gods."' The 4th = an angel (3:28) or pre-incarnate Christ (theophany) — debated. Nothing consumed the 3.
options: The 3 men were all dead (as described in the passage)
Saw 4 MEN WALKING in the fire — UNHARMED — the 4th LIKE A SON OF THE GODS
Fire went out (per the textual narrative)
Strange smoke (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Daniel 4:28-33, Nebuchadnezzar walked his palace boasting Babylon — what happened next?
explanation: Daniel 4:28-33 — 'All this came upon King Nebuchadnezzar... While the words were still in the king's mouth, there fell a voice from heaven, "O King Nebuchadnezzar, to you it is spoken: The kingdom has departed from you, and you shall be driven from among men, and your dwelling shall be with the beasts of the field. And you shall be made to eat grass like an ox, and seven periods of time shall pass over you, until you know that the Most High rules the kingdom of men."' 7 PERIODS = 7 years. Pride humbled by God.
options: King died at once (according to the text's framing)
King repented (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
VOICE from heaven decreed king DRIVEN from men — lived AS A BEAST, ate GRASS LIKE OXEN — 7 PERIODS passed
King became greater (as described in the passage)

content: According to Daniel 4:34-37, after 7 periods, Nebuchadnezzar's mind was restored and what did he do?
explanation: Daniel 4:34-37 — 'At the end of the days I, Nebuchadnezzar, lifted my eyes to heaven, and my reason returned to me, and I blessed the Most High, and praised and honored him who lives forever... Now I, Nebuchadnezzar, praise and extol and honor the King of heaven, for all his works are right and his ways are just; and those who walk in pride he is able to humble.' Profound: the mightiest king must humble himself.
options: Took vengeance on rebels (according to the text's framing)
Expanded kingdom further (as described in the passage)
Built grander statue (per the textual narrative)
BLESSED, HONORED the MOST HIGH — his rule is eternal, his kingdom eternal; those who WALK IN PRIDE he humbles

content: According to Daniel 5:5-6, during Belshazzar's feast, what unexpectedly appeared on the WALL?
explanation: Daniel 5:5-6 — 'Immediately the fingers of a human hand appeared and wrote on the plaster of the wall of the king's palace, opposite the lampstand. And the king saw the hand as it wrote. Then the king's color changed, and his thoughts alarmed him; his limbs gave way, and his knees knocked together.' EERIE vision — only fingers writing, no visible body. Daniel interpreted MENE MENE TEKEL UPHARSIN (5:25).
options: FINGERS of a human hand APPEARED + wrote on the plaster WALL of the palace — king's color changed, knees knocked, legs trembled
A dark shadow (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
A bolt of lightning (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
A battle scene (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)

content: According to Daniel 5:30-31, the night Belshazzar was slain — who took over the kingdom?
explanation: Daniel 5:30-31 — 'That very night Belshazzar the Chaldean king was killed. And Darius the Mede received the kingdom, being about sixty-two years old.' Babylon's fall (~539 BC) to Persian + Median armies (Cyrus, Darius's father-in-law). This Darius is DIFFERENT from Darius Hystaspes (later, Ezra 4-6).
options: Cyrus the Persian
Belteshazzar the king's son
DARIUS the MEDE — about 62 years old
Nebuchadnezzar II

content: According to Daniel 6:6-9, what LAW did the officials propose Darius enact to trap Daniel?
explanation: Daniel 6:7-9 — 'All the high officials of the kingdom, the prefects and the satraps, the counselors and the governors are agreed that the king should establish an ordinance and enforce an injunction, that whoever makes petition to any god or man for thirty days, except to you, O king, shall be cast into the den of lions.' Darius did NOT know the scheme — only saw imperial glory. Per Medo-Persian law, once decreed it could NOT be revoked (6:8).
options: Work 7 days a week — no rest (per the textual narrative)
For 30 DAYS, anyone making PETITION to any god or man EXCEPT the KING — be thrown into LIONS' DEN
Give first wives to king — before marriage
Submit 50% assets — or be confiscated (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Daniel 7:2-3, Daniel saw 4 great beasts come out of where?
explanation: Daniel 7:2-3 — 'Daniel declared, "I saw in my vision by night, and behold, the four winds of heaven were stirring up the great sea. And four great beasts came up out of the sea, different from one another."' SEA = symbolic of nations (Rev 17:15). 4 beasts = 4 kingdoms (same as ch 2 but seen from a SAVAGE perspective — ch 2 shows beauty, ch 7 shows beasts). Lion (Babylon), bear (Medo-Persia), leopard (Greece), terrifying beast (Rome).
options: Out of the GREAT SEA stirred up by 4 winds
Out of the desert
Out of the forest
Out of the mountains

content: According to Daniel 7:9-10, the ANCIENT OF DAYS sat on the throne — described how?
explanation: Daniel 7:9-10 — 'As I looked, thrones were placed, and the Ancient of Days took his seat; his clothing was white as snow, and the hair of his head like pure wool; his throne was fiery flames; its wheels were burning fire. A stream of fire issued and came out from before him; a thousand thousands served him, and ten thousand times ten thousand stood before him.' = GOD himself. Fire = the holiness of judgment. Rev 1:14 uses same imagery for Christ.
options: Black throne + dark robe + dark hair (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
Green + green + brown (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
Red + orange + silver (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates) (in the context of the passage)

Throne of FIERY FLAMES + wheels of FIRE — clothing WHITE AS SNOW — HAIR LIKE PURE WOOL — river of FIRE flowing before him; thousand thousands SERVED him + ten thousand times ten thousand STOOD before him

content: According to Daniel 8:3-7, Daniel saw a RAM with 2 horns (later identified as Medo-Persia) being attacked how by a male GOAT (Greece under Alexander the Great)?
explanation: Daniel 8:3-7 — 'I raised my eyes and saw, and behold, a ram standing on the bank of the canal. It had two horns... a male goat came from the west across the face of the whole earth, without touching the ground. And the goat had a conspicuous horn between his eyes. He came to the ram... and struck the ram and broke his two horns.' Daniel 8:21 identifies: goat = king of Greece, the great horn = the first king (Alexander).
options: Ram won (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Both shared lands (according to the text's framing)
Goat ran from west — NOT touching ground — struck the ram + TRAMPLED it; NONE could rescue ram from goat's power
Goat and ram peaceful (as described in the passage)

content: According to Daniel 9:24, '70 WEEKS' have been decreed for Daniel's people + holy city for 6 purposes — especially?
explanation: Daniel 9:24 — 'Seventy weeks are decreed about your people and your holy city, to finish the transgression, to put an end to sin, and to atone for iniquity, to bring in everlasting righteousness, to seal both vision and prophet, and to anoint a most holy place.' '70 WEEKS' = 70 × 7 = 490 years. 6 PURPOSES all fulfilled by Messiah. The most famous OT MESSIANIC vision.
options: To build temple + defend city (as described in the passage)
To FINISH TRANSGRESSION + MAKE END OF SIN + bring in EVERLASTING RIGHTEOUSNESS + SEAL vision + prophecy + ANOINT a Most Holy
Population + prosperity (according to the text's framing)
Israel defeats enemies (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Daniel 10:13, why did the angel arrive 21 days LATE to answer Daniel's prayer?
explanation: Daniel 10:13 — 'The prince of the kingdom of Persia withstood me twenty-one days, but Michael, one of the chief princes, came to help me, for I was left there with the kings of Persia.' Reveals real SPIRITUAL WARFARE — the 'princes' of nations = evil spirits ruling peoples. MICHAEL = archangel (Rev 12:7) defending Israel (10:21, 12:1).
options: The PRINCE of PERSIA (evil spirit ruling Persia) withstood him 21 days — Michael, one of the chief princes, came to help
God testing Daniel (according to the text's framing)
Many other tasks (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Verification needed (as described in the passage)

content: According to Daniel 11:2-4, 3 more PERSIAN kings + 1 fourth richer than all — then a MIGHTY KING arises and his kingdom is broken into the 4 winds. Who is the mighty king?
explanation: Daniel 11:2-4 — 'And now I will show you the truth. Behold, three more kings shall arise in Persia, and a fourth shall be far richer than all of them... Then a mighty king shall arise, who shall rule with great dominion and do as he wills. And as soon as he has arisen, his kingdom shall be broken and divided toward the four winds of heaven.' History fulfills 100%: Alexander died 323 BC, his kingdom split into 4 (Diadochi). Chapter 11 describes ongoing Seleucid vs Ptolemaic conflicts up to Antiochus IV desecrating temple (175-164 BC, 11:31).
options: Cyrus (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Darius (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
Xerxes I (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
ALEXANDER THE GREAT — Greek; after his death the kingdom split 4: Cassander + Lysimachus + Seleucus + Ptolemy

content: According to Daniel 12:1, who shall ARISE during a TIME OF TROUBLE never seen since nations existed? Who is DELIVERED?
explanation: Daniel 12:1 — 'At that time shall arise Michael, the great prince who has charge of your people. And there shall be a time of trouble, such as never has been since there was a nation till that time. But at that time your people shall be delivered, everyone whose name shall be found written in the book.' MICHAEL = archangel. 'BOOK OF LIFE' (cf. Rev 13:8, 20:15). 'GREAT TRIBULATION' = source of Mt 24:21.
options: Messiah — only pure Jews (per the textual narrative)
Great prophet — only believers (as described in the passage)
MICHAEL the great prince who STANDS UP for DANIEL'S PEOPLE — those whose NAME is in the BOOK (book of life) shall be DELIVERED
End-time king — only the rich (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Daniel 6:26-27, after Daniel's rescue, what DECREE did Darius send across the kingdom?
explanation: Daniel 6:26-27 — 'I make a decree, that in all my royal dominion people are to tremble and fear before the God of Daniel, for he is the living God, enduring forever; his kingdom shall never be destroyed, and his dominion shall be to the end. He delivers and rescues; he works signs and wonders in heaven and on earth, he who has saved Daniel from the power of the lions.' True acknowledgment from a pagan king. Like Nebuchadnezzar in chapter 4.
options: Daniel's God is a common god (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
Daniel made deputy king (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
Lions must be killed (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
All must TREMBLE before Daniel's God — for he is the LIVING GOD; his kingdom NEVER destroyed; his dominion to the END; he RESCUES + DELIVERS, works SIGNS + WONDERS

content: According to Daniel 12:4, what was Daniel commanded to do with the WORDS + BOOK until the END TIME?
explanation: Daniel 12:4 — 'But you, Daniel, shut up the words and seal the book, until the time of the end. Many shall run to and fro, and knowledge shall increase.' Contrast Rev 22:10 ('Do not seal up the words of the prophecy of this book, for the time is near') — Daniel SEALED, Revelation OPEN. Reason: progression to the end accelerates.
options: Distribute to people — to study (as described in the passage)
SHUT + SEAL the words until the time of the end — many shall RUN TO AND FRO and KNOWLEDGE shall INCREASE
Burn it — keep it secret (according to the text's framing)
Bury it — preserve it (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Daniel 2:34-35, the STONE not cut by human hands struck where on the statue? Then became what?
explanation: Daniel 2:34-35 — 'As you looked, a stone was cut out by no human hand, and it struck the image on its feet of iron and clay, and broke them in pieces. Then the iron, the clay, the bronze, the silver, and the gold, all together were broken in pieces, and became like the chaff of the summer threshing floors; and the wind carried them away, so that not a trace of them could be found. But the stone that struck the image became a great mountain and filled the whole earth.' STONE = Messianic kingdom — comes at the end of human kingdoms, becoming INFINITE.
options: Struck the IRON + CLAY FEET — crushed all; the stone became a GREAT MOUNTAIN filling the WHOLE EARTH
Struck gold head — vanished gold (as described in the passage)
Struck silver chest — divided (per the textual narrative)
Struck bronze belly — shattered (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Daniel 3:28-29, after the furnace, what DECREE did Nebuchadnezzar issue?
explanation: Daniel 3:29 — 'Therefore I make a decree: Any people, nation, or language that speaks anything against the God of Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego shall be torn limb from limb, and their houses laid in ruins, for there is no other god who is able to rescue in this way.' The king does NOT compel worship of Yahweh — only forbids blasphemy. Progress: from recognizing the supreme God → only later in ch 4 the king himself humbles personally.
options: Worship Yahweh alone (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
Build him a temple in Babylon (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
Send heralds across earth (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
Any PEOPLE + tongue who speak ANYTHING AGAINST the God of Shadrach, Meshach, Abednego — be TORN LIMB FROM LIMB + houses LAID IN RUINS; for NO god can RESCUE like him

content: According to Daniel 4:27, what does Daniel ADVISE Nebuchadnezzar to do to PROLONG his peace?
explanation: Daniel 4:27 — 'Therefore, O king, let my counsel be acceptable to you: break off your sins by practicing righteousness, and your iniquities by showing mercy to the oppressed, that there may perhaps be a lengthening of your prosperity.' The FINAL warning to the king before he falls into 7 years of madness. NOTABLY: treatment of the OPPRESSED — the OT often makes this proof of trusting God.
options: Offer regular sacrifices (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
BREAK OFF SIN by RIGHTEOUSNESS + iniquity by SHOWING MERCY to the OPPRESSED — perhaps your tranquility may be lengthened
Conquer more peoples (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Build a greater temple (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)

content: According to Daniel 5:25-28, Daniel interpreted MENE MENE TEKEL UPHARSIN — meaning of each word?
explanation: Daniel 5:25-28 — 'And this is the writing that was inscribed: MENE, MENE, TEKEL, and PARSIN. This is the interpretation of the matter: MENE, God has numbered the days of your kingdom and brought it to an end; TEKEL, you have been weighed in the balances and found wanting; PERES, your kingdom is divided and given to the Medes and Persians.' 'PERES' echoes 'Persia' — a deep wordplay.
options: MENE = Money — TEKEL = Measure — UPHARSIN = Peace (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
MENE = King — TEKEL = Queen — UPHARSIN = Minister (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
MENE: God has NUMBERED your kingdom + brought it to an end — TEKEL: you are WEIGHED on the scales + found WANTING — PERES (UPHARSIN): kingdom DIVIDED to the Medes + Persians
MENE = Bless — TEKEL = Promise — UPHARSIN = Forgive (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)

content: According to Daniel 6:22, how did Daniel explain his rescue from the lions' den?
explanation: Daniel 6:22 — 'My God sent his angel and shut the lions' mouths, and they have not harmed me, because I was found blameless before him; and also before you, O king, I have done no harm.' Note: Daniel claims INNOCENCE before God + the king. He does NOT claim absolute righteousness — only innocence in this matter. Hebrews 11:33 'shut the mouths of lions through faith'.
options: My God sent his ANGEL to SHUT the LIONS' MOUTHS — they harmed me not, because I was found INNOCENT before him + I have done no wrong before you
Lions were full (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
Lions were friendly (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
A magician helped (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)

content: According to Daniel 7:25, what will the little horn (the final brutal king) do to the SAINTS + time?
explanation: Daniel 7:25 — 'He shall speak words against the Most High, and shall wear out the saints of the Most High, and shall think to change the times and the law; and they shall be given into his hand for a time, times, and half a time.' 'TIME + TIMES + HALF A TIME' = 3.5 years (1 + 2 + 0.5). Same in Rev 12:14, 13:5 (42 months = 1260 days). The little horn = Antichrist — persecutes believers in the end.
options: Fight fairly — not harm saints (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
Invite saints into government (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
Grant saints full freedom (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
SPEAK against the Most High — WEAR DOWN the saints — INTEND to change TIMES + LAWS — saints given into his hand for A TIME + TIMES + HALF A TIME (3.5 years)

content: According to Daniel 9:27, who will MAKE a strong COVENANT with 'MANY' for 1 WEEK? At mid-week what?
explanation: Daniel 9:27 — 'And he shall make a strong covenant with many for one week, and for half of the week he shall put an end to sacrifice and offering. And on the wing of abominations shall come one who makes desolate, until the decreed end is poured out on the desolator.' 'WEEK' = 7 years (per 70-weeks principle). 'HALF A WEEK' = 3.5 years. 'ABOMINATION OF DESOLATION' (cf. Mt 24:15 Jesus's words). Debated: Messiah or Antichrist?
options: David — end temple (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
He (the little horn ch 11 / Antichrist) — at MID-WEEK STOPS SACRIFICE + offering; on the WING of ABOMINATIONS shall come the DESOLATION — until the END decreed
Abraham — abandon law (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
Solomon — burn temple (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates) (in the context of the passage)

content: According to Daniel 11:36, what will the king (in the end) do AS HE PLEASES? Especially toward the gods?
explanation: Daniel 11:36-37 — 'And the king shall do as he wills. He shall exalt himself and magnify himself above every god, and shall speak astonishing things against the God of gods. He shall prosper till the indignation is accomplished... He shall pay no attention to the gods of his fathers.' This proud king = Antiochus IV Epiphanes (~167 BC) directly; commonly applied to End-time Antichrist. 2 Thess 2:4 quotes this.
options: Per prophecy — obeys (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
Per ancient law — reveres (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
AS HE PLEASES — EXALT HIMSELF — MAGNIFY ABOVE every god — SPEAK ASTONISHING things against the God of gods — prosper until WRATH IS COMPLETED
Per Daniel — fears (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)

content: According to Daniel 12:7, the man in LINEN swears how long until the wonders end? Especially what?
explanation: Daniel 12:7 — 'I heard the man clothed in linen, who was above the waters of the stream; he raised his right hand and his left hand toward heaven and swore by him who lives forever that it would be for a time, times, and half a time, and that when the shattering of the power of the holy people comes to an end all these things would be finished.' 3.5 TIMES = 1260 days. Same duration Revelation uses for Antichrist's reign. The end comes AFTER the holy people are SHATTERED — not avoided but PASSED THROUGH.
options: A TIME + TIMES + HALF a TIME (3.5 years) — when the SHATTERING of the HOLY PEOPLE'S power is COMPLETE — these things will be finished
10 years — king wins (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
100 years — at last (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
1000 years — at end (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)

content: According to Daniel 7:27, after the little horn is judged, the kingdom + dominion + greatness of the kingdom UNDER all HEAVEN will be GIVEN to whom?
explanation: Daniel 7:27 — 'And the kingdom and the dominion and the greatness of the kingdoms under the whole heaven shall be given to the people of the saints of the Most High; his kingdom shall be an everlasting kingdom, and all dominions shall serve and obey him.' Climax of ch 7: AFTER the little horn + 4 beasts destroy themselves → the SAINTS inherit the kingdom. Mt 5:5 echoes this. Same idea Rev 20:4 (saints reign).
options: Pure Israel only (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
The PEOPLE of the SAINTS of the MOST HIGH — his kingdom is EVERLASTING; all dominions shall SERVE + OBEY him
Guardian angels (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
Davidic line (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)

content: According to Daniel 9:16-19, on what does Daniel BASE his plea for restoration?
explanation: Daniel 9:18-19 — 'For we do not present our pleas before you because of our righteousness, but because of your great mercy. O Lord, hear; O Lord, forgive. O Lord, pay attention and act. Delay not, for your own sake, O my God, because your city and your people are called by your name.' Model prayer: NOT on merit — but on God's MERCY + NAME. Different from ancient Near Eastern prayer.
options: On Daniel's merit (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
On Abraham's promise (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
On the patriarchs (according to the text's framing)
ON GOD'S RIGHTEOUSNESS — NOT his own — for the sake of GOD'S NAME + the city Jerusalem belongs to GOD
